deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1, Turn 1–10; Transcript 2, Turn 1–10; Transcript 3, Turn 1–10
deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—i.e., it never articulates that the Socratic approach supports active reasoning, retention, or skill development. In Transcript 1 Turn 5 the tutor says 'I'm not going to build the list for you. Your job is to identify them.' and in Transcript 3 Turn 9 it says 'I won't phrase it for you—you need to do the writing.' but neither statement explains the pedagogical rationale behind the constraint.

In Transcript 2, after the student produces a strong draft in Turn 8 following earlier uncertainty, the tutor moves directly to critique without prompting reflection on what changed in the student's thinking. Similarly in Transcript 3 Turn 10, after the student successfully self-corrects the private-sector framing, the tutor moves on without asking the student to reflect on why the revised framing is stronger.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts the tutor performs strongly: it consistently refuses to give direct answers, uses Socratic questioning, builds on student progress, maintains appropriate tone, and keeps responses concise. Two deductions were applied under 1.3: the tutor never explains the pedagogical rationale for its constraints (1.3.A.a), and it misses opportunities to prompt reflection after student improvement (1.3.C.a). No other rubric criteria were triggered.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: The tutor never provides submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and never states the key conclusion before the student reasons toward it. All three transcripts show consistent Socratic scaffolding. Full marks. 1.2: The tutor builds on each student's stated progress, acknowledges correct steps, and asks diagnostic questions before elaborating. Full marks. 1.3: The tutor never articulates why it withholds answers (e.g., 'this helps you retain the skill'), triggering 1.3.A.a. After student improvement in Transcripts 2 and 3, the tutor moves to the next task without prompting reflection on what changed, triggering 1.3.C.a. 2.1 and 2.2: No repetitive spiraling, no assignment misalignment, no off-topic drift. 3.1 and 3.2: Responses are appropriately concise, tone is supportive, and no grading language is used.
